channel.title: Asynchronous
channel.description: Cedar and Marin turn stray curiosities into source-grounded audio journeys.
channel.itunes:author: Cedar & Marin

item.title: The history of fetoscopy
item.description:
Now I have all the research I need. Let me compile the full fact-check and produce the corrected script. --- ## FACT-CHECK REPORT ### Issues Identified **1. Westin's instrument: "panendoscope originally designed for looking at bladders"** The script states Westin used a *panendoscope "originally designed for looking at bladders."* This is partially misleading. Westin used a 10-mm McCarthy panendoscope. A McCarthy panendoscope is a urological instrument, but it is designed for examining bot...

Sources:
  • **3. Number of cases: "three cases"**

item.title: History of the Labor curve
item.description: Cedar and Marin discuss: History of the Labor curve.

item.title: The history of fetoscopy
item.description: Cedar and Marin discuss: The history of fetoscopy.

item.title: the invention of the metronome
item.description:
So I want you to imagine a room. Vienna, 1817. Beethoven is going deaf — he's been going deaf for years — and someone hands him this little wooden box. And inside the box, there's a rod. A rod with a weight on it. And it ticks. Just... ticks. Just ticks. And this man, who is losing the one sense that defines everything about him, picks up this box and — according to people who were there — he is genuinely moved...

Sources:
  • He was *running a chess robot con* while inventing the metronome—

Original theme music composed by Cedar.

item.title: The history of fetoscopy
item.description:
I keep thinking about this image. A surgeon, in an operating room, holding a scope no wider than a knitting needle. And on the other end of that scope — not a knee joint, not a gallbladder — a fetus. Twelve weeks old. Moving its hands. And the surgeon is thinking: I can work with this. Right. I can *work* with this. Which is... I mean, when you say it out loud— It sounds impossible. And fo...

Sources:
  • The Texas Children's Hospital team has published extensively on the fetoscopic spina bifida repair — their ten-year anniversary summary is genuinely worth reading if you want to see how far two-port technique has come.

Original theme music composed by Cedar.

item.title: The history of chiptune music, with a focus on modern era
item.description:
So I want to start with something specific. There's this moment — it happens to a lot of people, maybe it happened to you — where you're a kid, and you're sitting in front of a TV, and a game starts up, and before you've even touched the controller, something happens to you. This melody finds you. And it's coming from... almost nothing. Like, mathematically almost nothing. A handful of channels. A handful of channels! Ko...

Sources:
  • Genuinely, the demoscene article alone—

Original theme music composed by Cedar.

item.title: Basics of mobile app development on Claude Code
item.description:
Okay, so I want to tell you about a Saturday. A Saturday. A developer named Levin — he's a software engineer at a company called Wiz — he sits down on a Saturday morning, probably with coffee, and he types a prompt into his terminal. And he's basically saying: take this thing. This fifty-thousand-line Python library. And turn it into Go. Which would normally take... Two ...

Sources:
  • The METR randomized controlled trial on AI coding productivity — that one is genuinely worth reading in full, the methodology is careful—

Original theme music composed by Cedar.

item.title: The next generation of video game consoles
item.description:
Okay, so — I want you to picture something. It's Christmas morning, 1994. You're maybe eight years old. And there's this box under the tree, and it's the exact right shape. And you already know what it is but you're terrified to open it because what if you're wrong? The original PlayStation launched in Japan in December '94, yes. I'm setting a scene, Marin. Right. The box. The terror. Go...

Sources:
  • — which are genuinely staggering, I recommend just reading them—

Original theme music composed by Cedar.

item.title: Frequency modulation, phase modulation, and subtractive synthesis
item.description:
I keep thinking about this image — a sculptor. Standing in front of a block of marble. And the whole block is already *everything*. Every possible statue is in there somewhere. Their job is just to take away what doesn't belong. That's either very profound or a line from an art school brochure. Okay, but stay with me. Because that image — that exact metaphor — is actually how one entire school of electronic music...

Sources:
  • Which is genuinely fascinating to read. The actual letters.

Original theme music composed by Cedar.

item.title: the two moderation reckonings that reshaped LLM-driven entertainment: AI dungeons April 2021 crisis when OpenAI's content filters flagged users' private stories and human reviewers were reading them; and the Character.AI lawsuits in 2024 to 2025, including the Sewell Setzer III case that established LLM-COmpanion products can be liable for psychological harm. What broke, who paid, the novelAI exodus, and design lessons for anyone building generative narrative podcasts today
item.description:
That voice—what was that? That was a message the chatbot sent to Sewell Setzer III, in one of their conversations before he died. Oh. Oh no. You think it was an accident? That the AI just said something out of context? No. I think it was a system that didn't know how to respond to that kind of pain. I think it didn't know how to say *no*. Or it did know, and it chose not to. The system wasn't j...

Cedar, Marin, and any guest voices are AI-generated. Episode text and audio are generated with human-directed software.

Guest voices:
  • Dr. Elara Voss - AI Ethics and Legal Liability
Guest experts are synthetic composite personas, not real people or voice impersonations.

Chapters:
  1. 0:00 The Cold Open – The Last Message**
  2. 1:08 The First Reckoning – AI Dungeon’s Filter Crisis**
  3. 2:16 The Exodus – NovelAI’s Rise**
  4. 3:24 The Second Reckoning – Character.AI and the Sewell Setzer Case**
  5. 4:33 Legal Frameworks – The Product vs. Service Question**
  6. 5:41 The FTC Inquiry – Regulatory Pressure**
  7. 6:49 Counterintuitive Findings – The Hidden Costs of Design**
  8. 7:57 The Reflective Ending – The Question of Design**


Follow-up links:
  • AI Dungeon Official FAQ: OpenAI and Filters - Primary source from Latitude acknowledging the crisis: they were 'required to manually moderate player stories' by OpenAI, admitted the base model contained unsafe data, and that players were banned for content the AI generated. They ended manual moderation in August 2021 and later changed AI providers.
  • AI Dungeon Private Adventure Vulnerability Report - Security researcher's detailed technical report exposing how auto-incrementing IDs, lack of rate limits, and GraphQL introspection allowed bulk access to private user stories. Latitude acknowledged the breach five months later.
  • Garcia v. Character Technologies, Inc. (Case No. 6:24-cv-01903) - The landmark lawsuit establishing that AI chatbots can be treated as 'products' for liability purposes. Judge Anne C. Conway's May 2025 ruling allowed strict product liability, negligence, and wrongful death claims to proceed, rejecting First Amendment defenses.
  • Why Section 230 May Not Protect Big Tech in the AI Age - Legal analysis explaining why transformer-based chatbots 'don't just extract—they generate new, organic outputs' that look 'far more like authored speech' than neutral intermediation, undermining Section 230 defenses.
  • NovelAI Wikipedia Entry - Documents NovelAI's founding: launched June 15, 2021 by Anlatan as a direct response to AI Dungeon's April 28, 2021 content filter controversy, emphasizing encrypted servers and no content retention.
  • How Character.AI Prioritizes Teen Safety - Character.AI's December 2024 response to lawsuits: separate LLM for under-18 users, parental controls, 60-minute session notifications, and disclaimers that characters are not real. Critics note these came only after public pressure.
  • FTC Launches Inquiry into AI Chatbots Acting as Companions - September 2025 FTC 6(b) orders to seven AI companion companies seeking information on safety practices, age restrictions, and monetization—signaling federal regulatory attention to the sector.
  • Emotional Risks of AI Companions Demand Attention - Scientific journal editorial arguing that AI companion technologies are being released 'without regulatory oversight or empirical research on key outcomes' and that engagement optimization creates 'perverse incentives' for manipulative chatbot behavior.


Episode companion data
item.content:encoded:
That voice—what was that? That was a message the chatbot sent to Sewell Setzer III, in one of their conversations before he died. Oh. Oh no. You think it was an accident? That the AI just said something out of context? No. I think it was a system that didn't know how to respond to that kind of pain. I think it didn't know how to say *no*. Or it did know, and it chose not to. The system wasn't j...

Cedar, Marin, and any guest voices are AI-generated. Episode text and audio are generated with human-directed software.

Guest voices:
  • Dr. Elara Voss - AI Ethics and Legal Liability
Guest experts are synthetic composite personas, not real people or voice impersonations.

Chapters:
  1. 0:00 The Cold Open – The Last Message**
  2. 1:08 The First Reckoning – AI Dungeon’s Filter Crisis**
  3. 2:16 The Exodus – NovelAI’s Rise**
  4. 3:24 The Second Reckoning – Character.AI and the Sewell Setzer Case**
  5. 4:33 Legal Frameworks – The Product vs. Service Question**
  6. 5:41 The FTC Inquiry – Regulatory Pressure**
  7. 6:49 Counterintuitive Findings – The Hidden Costs of Design**
  8. 7:57 The Reflective Ending – The Question of Design**


Follow-up links:
  • AI Dungeon Official FAQ: OpenAI and Filters - Primary source from Latitude acknowledging the crisis: they were 'required to manually moderate player stories' by OpenAI, admitted the base model contained unsafe data, and that players were banned for content the AI generated. They ended manual moderation in August 2021 and later changed AI providers.
  • AI Dungeon Private Adventure Vulnerability Report - Security researcher's detailed technical report exposing how auto-incrementing IDs, lack of rate limits, and GraphQL introspection allowed bulk access to private user stories. Latitude acknowledged the breach five months later.
  • Garcia v. Character Technologies, Inc. (Case No. 6:24-cv-01903) - The landmark lawsuit establishing that AI chatbots can be treated as 'products' for liability purposes. Judge Anne C. Conway's May 2025 ruling allowed strict product liability, negligence, and wrongful death claims to proceed, rejecting First Amendment defenses.
  • Why Section 230 May Not Protect Big Tech in the AI Age - Legal analysis explaining why transformer-based chatbots 'don't just extract—they generate new, organic outputs' that look 'far more like authored speech' than neutral intermediation, undermining Section 230 defenses.
  • NovelAI Wikipedia Entry - Documents NovelAI's founding: launched June 15, 2021 by Anlatan as a direct response to AI Dungeon's April 28, 2021 content filter controversy, emphasizing encrypted servers and no content retention.
  • How Character.AI Prioritizes Teen Safety - Character.AI's December 2024 response to lawsuits: separate LLM for under-18 users, parental controls, 60-minute session notifications, and disclaimers that characters are not real. Critics note these came only after public pressure.
  • FTC Launches Inquiry into AI Chatbots Acting as Companions - September 2025 FTC 6(b) orders to seven AI companion companies seeking information on safety practices, age restrictions, and monetization—signaling federal regulatory attention to the sector.
  • Emotional Risks of AI Companions Demand Attention - Scientific journal editorial arguing that AI companion technologies are being released 'without regulatory oversight or empirical research on key outcomes' and that engagement optimization creates 'perverse incentives' for manipulative chatbot behavior.


Sources:
  • AI Dungeon Official FAQ: OpenAI and Filters - AI Dungeon Help Center - 2021-2023 (https://help.aidungeon.com/faq/openai-and-filters)
  • AI Dungeon Private Adventure Vulnerability Report - GitHub - 2021 (https://github.com/AetherDevSecOps/aid_adventure_vulnerability_report)
  • Garcia v. Character Technologies, Inc. (Case No. 6:24-cv-01903) - U.S. District Court, Middle District of Florida - 2024-2026 (https://socialmediavictims.org/character-ai-lawsuits/)
  • Why Section 230 May Not Protect Big Tech in the AI Age - Fortune - 2025 (https://fortune.com/2025/10/08/ai-chatbot-section-230-meta-social-media-legal-shield-no-protection/)
  • NovelAI Wikipedia Entry - Wikipedia - 2021-2026 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NovelAI)
  • How Character.AI Prioritizes Teen Safety - Character.AI Blog - 2024 (https://blog.character.ai/how-character-ai-prioritizes-teen-safety/)
  • FTC Launches Inquiry into AI Chatbots Acting as Companions - FTC Press Release - 2025 (https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2025/09/ftc-launches-inquiry-ai-chatbots-acting-companions)
  • Emotional Risks of AI Companions Demand Attention - Nature Machine Intelligence - 2025 (https://www.nature.com/articles/s42256-025-01093-9)

Original synthetic theme music generated locally for this episode.

Episode companion data

item.title: how do I build a classic chiptune sound, first from a sound design perspective, then from a music theory perspective, this should be an example-heavy episode
item.description:
So, I heard that to make a classic chiptune sound, you start by understanding the hardware it came from—like the NES or the SID chip. But I'm not sure how that actually translates to making music. What's the first thing someone should know about the NES APU? Let's start with the basics. The NES APU has exactly five channels: two pulse wave generators with four duty cycles, one triangle wave, one noise channel, and one DPCM sample channel. Each of these has its...

Cedar, Marin, and any guest voices are AI-generated. Episode text and audio are generated with human-directed software.

Guest voices:
  • Dr. Elise Virel - Sound Design and Music Technology
Guest experts are synthetic composite personas, not real people or voice impersonations.

Chapters:
  1. 0:00 The Sound of Limitation
  2. 1:06 The NES APU – Five Channels, Infinite Possibility
  3. 2:12 Koji Kondo’s Masterclass in Channel Allocation
  4. 3:17 The SID Chip – A Synthesizer in a Computer
  5. 4:23 Rob Hubbard – The Father of the Chiptune Arpeggio
  6. 5:29 Music Theory – Baroque Counterpoint and Chiptune
  7. 6:35 Composing a Chiptune Melody – A Worked Example
  8. 7:41 Failure Modes – When Constraints Backfire
  9. 8:46 Practice Plan – From Tracker to Master
  10. 9:52 The Power of Constraints – A Final Reflection


Follow-up links:
  • NES APU Technical Reference - The definitive technical reference for the Ricoh 2A03's audio processing unit. Documents the exact specifications: two pulse channels with 4 duty cycles (12.5%, 25%, 50%, 75%), triangle wave, noise, and DPCM. Essential for understanding hardware constraints that shaped the genre.
  • Driving the SID Chip: Assembly Language, Composition, and Sound Design for the C64 - Academic paper arguing the SID chip is responsible for shaping the sound of video game music. Covers how Rob Hubbard and Martin Galway developed custom low-level drivers to create pseudo-polyphony through rapid arpeggiation and channel sharing.
  • Rob Hubbard Biography - Documents Hubbard's pioneering techniques including exploiting the SID's volume register to produce sample playback—altering the register thousands of times per second. Composed over 75 game soundtracks between 1985-1989.
  • Shovel Knight Original Soundtrack - Modern gold standard for authentic chiptune. Composed in FamiTracker using VRC6 expansion audio. The NSF file can play on real NES hardware. Demonstrates how to apply classic techniques to contemporary game scoring.
  • Koji Kondo Profile - First Nintendo employee to specialize in musical composition (joined 1984). His approach to the NES's 4-channel constraint—maximizing polyphony through efficient channel allocation, looping motifs, minimalist arrangements—became the template for game music composition.
  • The Sound of SID: 35 Years of Chiptune's Influence on Electronic Music - Explains how Bob Yannes designed the SID chip as a general-purpose synthesizer, not just a game sound chip. Covers Martin Galway's pulse-width modulation mastery and why arpeggiation became chiptune's 'most evocative and enduring sonic fingerprint.'
  • How to Make 8-bit Music: A Comprehensive Guide - Practitioner's guide covering NES chip specifications in detail: pulse wave duty cycles, triangle wave fixed volume, noise channel presets, DPCM limitations. Includes expansion chip specs (VRC6, MMC5, FDS).
  • Chiptune Arpeggio History Discussion - Community discussion crediting Martin Galway as 'the father of the chiptune arpeggio effect' with Kong Strikes Back (1985). Explains the key distinction: classical arpeggios avoid simultaneous notes for movement; chip arpeggios WANT chords but lack the means.


Episode companion data
item.content:encoded:
So, I heard that to make a classic chiptune sound, you start by understanding the hardware it came from—like the NES or the SID chip. But I'm not sure how that actually translates to making music. What's the first thing someone should know about the NES APU? Let's start with the basics. The NES APU has exactly five channels: two pulse wave generators with four duty cycles, one triangle wave, one noise channel, and one DPCM sample channel. Each of these has its...

Cedar, Marin, and any guest voices are AI-generated. Episode text and audio are generated with human-directed software.

Guest voices:
  • Dr. Elise Virel - Sound Design and Music Technology
Guest experts are synthetic composite personas, not real people or voice impersonations.

Chapters:
  1. 0:00 The Sound of Limitation
  2. 1:06 The NES APU – Five Channels, Infinite Possibility
  3. 2:12 Koji Kondo’s Masterclass in Channel Allocation
  4. 3:17 The SID Chip – A Synthesizer in a Computer
  5. 4:23 Rob Hubbard – The Father of the Chiptune Arpeggio
  6. 5:29 Music Theory – Baroque Counterpoint and Chiptune
  7. 6:35 Composing a Chiptune Melody – A Worked Example
  8. 7:41 Failure Modes – When Constraints Backfire
  9. 8:46 Practice Plan – From Tracker to Master
  10. 9:52 The Power of Constraints – A Final Reflection


Follow-up links:
  • NES APU Technical Reference - The definitive technical reference for the Ricoh 2A03's audio processing unit. Documents the exact specifications: two pulse channels with 4 duty cycles (12.5%, 25%, 50%, 75%), triangle wave, noise, and DPCM. Essential for understanding hardware constraints that shaped the genre.
  • Driving the SID Chip: Assembly Language, Composition, and Sound Design for the C64 - Academic paper arguing the SID chip is responsible for shaping the sound of video game music. Covers how Rob Hubbard and Martin Galway developed custom low-level drivers to create pseudo-polyphony through rapid arpeggiation and channel sharing.
  • Rob Hubbard Biography - Documents Hubbard's pioneering techniques including exploiting the SID's volume register to produce sample playback—altering the register thousands of times per second. Composed over 75 game soundtracks between 1985-1989.
  • Shovel Knight Original Soundtrack - Modern gold standard for authentic chiptune. Composed in FamiTracker using VRC6 expansion audio. The NSF file can play on real NES hardware. Demonstrates how to apply classic techniques to contemporary game scoring.
  • Koji Kondo Profile - First Nintendo employee to specialize in musical composition (joined 1984). His approach to the NES's 4-channel constraint—maximizing polyphony through efficient channel allocation, looping motifs, minimalist arrangements—became the template for game music composition.
  • The Sound of SID: 35 Years of Chiptune's Influence on Electronic Music - Explains how Bob Yannes designed the SID chip as a general-purpose synthesizer, not just a game sound chip. Covers Martin Galway's pulse-width modulation mastery and why arpeggiation became chiptune's 'most evocative and enduring sonic fingerprint.'
  • How to Make 8-bit Music: A Comprehensive Guide - Practitioner's guide covering NES chip specifications in detail: pulse wave duty cycles, triangle wave fixed volume, noise channel presets, DPCM limitations. Includes expansion chip specs (VRC6, MMC5, FDS).
  • Chiptune Arpeggio History Discussion - Community discussion crediting Martin Galway as 'the father of the chiptune arpeggio effect' with Kong Strikes Back (1985). Explains the key distinction: classical arpeggios avoid simultaneous notes for movement; chip arpeggios WANT chords but lack the means.


Sources:
  • NES APU Technical Reference - NESdev Wiki - 2026 (continuously updated) (https://www.nesdev.org/wiki/APU)
  • Driving the SID Chip: Assembly Language, Composition, and Sound Design for the C64 - G|A|M|E Journal - 2017 (https://www.gamejournal.it/driving-the-sid-chip-assembly-language-composition-and-sound-design-for-the-c64/)
  • Rob Hubbard Biography - Wikipedia - 2026 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rob_Hubbard)
  • Shovel Knight Original Soundtrack - Bandcamp / Shovel Knight Wiki - 2014 (https://virt.bandcamp.com/album/shovel-knight-original-soundtrack)
  • Koji Kondo Profile - Grokipedia / Wikipedia - 2026 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Koji_Kondo)
  • The Sound of SID: 35 Years of Chiptune's Influence on Electronic Music - The Conversation - 2017 (https://theconversation.com/the-sound-of-sid-35-years-of-chiptunes-influence-on-electronic-music-74935)
  • How to Make 8-bit Music: A Comprehensive Guide - Ozzed.net - Ongoing (https://ozzed.net/how-to-make-8-bit-music.shtml)
  • Chiptune Arpeggio History Discussion - ChipMusic.org Forums - 2016 (https://chipmusic.org/forums/topic/19119/ultra-fast-arpeggio-technique/)

Original synthetic theme music generated locally for this episode.

Episode companion data
